calibration.repoShape: TypeScript build tool/framework monorepo package sources with CLI lazy loading, config-file loading, module-runner imports, CSS preprocessor loaders, and optional legacy-plugin dependencies
calibration.safetyPosture: Clone-only source scan; no target installs, package-manager scripts, target tests, target builds, submodules, or npx were run.
calibration.scopeQuestion: Can Axiom's opt-in dynamic warning family stay useful on Vite package production sources by separating literal lazy imports from non-literal runtime/config/plugin loading expressions?
calibration.commandSurface: axi infer --json + axi graph --json + axi diff + axi observe --baseline
calibration.mainSignal: Vite package sources provide a second dynamic-heavy calibration after Nitro: five readable inferred source modules, stable package-source edges, and ten focused non-literal runtime/config/plugin loading expressions as advisory graph-completeness evidence.
calibration.decision: Record as comparison evidence for dynamic dependency warnings; keep them opt-in review evidence and do not change scanner, resolver, or gate behavior from this run.
calibration.codeChanged: No Axiom validator code changed; this is a no-install dynamic calibration run.
calibration.followUp: Use Nitro plus Vite together to calibrate dynamic warning documentation, but wait for at least one app/runtime-plugin repo or Python dynamic pilot before changing defaults.

reviewStory.summary: Starter contract inferred 5 modules and 5 observed module edges from 125 source files.
reviewStory.setup: Scanned 125 source files and 1255 imports; 5 candidate groups became 5 starter modules. This is a current-graph snapshot, not declared architecture intent yet.

pressures.title: Large-file pressure in inferred scope
pressures.description: 8 large source files may hide responsibilities that folder and import inference cannot split. Inspect these files before judging the starter module map as complete.

pressures.title: Review inferred dependencies
pressures.description: 5 observed module edges became `depends on` lines with sample import evidence. Confirm each edge is intended before using this draft as a gate.

reviewStory.nextStep: Inspect large-file pressure notes, adjust module boundaries if needed, then run `axi observe --root . --spec <draft.axi> --markdown`.
reviewStory.caveat: Inference reads static imports and folder/package shape. It can lower authoring cost, but humans still decide module names, visibility, layers, accepted debt, and which edges are real architecture intent.
